entries.title: IBM outlines Kookaburra / Blue Jay path toward 100k+ physical qubits
Quantinuum H2 trapped-ion system passes 56 physical qubits at high fidelity
IBM debuts its 133-qubit Heron processor and Quantum System Two, its first modular quantum computer
Atom Computing unveils a 1,225-site neutral-atom array populated with 1,180 qubits, the first gate-based system to exceed 1,000 qubits
Harvard, QuEra, MIT and NIST/University of Maryland run error-corrected algorithms on 48 logical qubits using a 280-atom neutral-atom array
Google's 105-qubit Willow chip demonstrates error correction below the surface-code threshold, with logical error rates falling as qubit count scales up
Microsoft unveils Majorana 1, its first quantum processor built with topological qubits, with 8 qubits on a chip designed to scale to one million
PsiQuantum unveils Omega, a photonic quantum computing chipset manufactured at a GlobalFoundries commercial semiconductor fab in New York
USTC's Jiuzhang 3.0 photonic quantum computer detects 255 photons in a Gaussian boson sampling experiment, claiming an advantage over classical supercomputers
Origin Quantum launches Wukong, China's first homegrown 72-qubit superconducting quantum computer, for public cloud access
China's Hefei-based superconducting quantum computer production line expands capacity to assemble 8 devices simultaneously, up from 5
entries.org: Anhui Quantum Computing Engineering Research Center

entries.title: China Telecom, CAS and QuantumCTek unveil Tianyan-504, a quantum computer powered by the 504-qubit Xiaohong superconducting chip
USTC's 105-qubit Zuchongzhi-3 processor claims a random circuit sampling speed roughly a quadrillion times faster than the fastest supercomputer
Oxford Quantum Circuits launches Toshiko, a 32-qubit platform deployed directly inside a commercial data centre
IQM announces its Radiance roadmap, targeting a 150-qubit quantum computer for enterprise and HPC customers
Pasqal loads more than 1,000 rubidium atoms in a single shot in its neutral-atom quantum processor
VTT and IQM launch Europe's first 50-qubit superconducting quantum computer at Micronova in Espoo, Finland
Alice & Bob reports cat-qubit bit-flip lifetimes exceeding one hour, up from its prior 430-second record on the Boson 4 chip
Fujitsu and RIKEN unveil a 256-qubit superconducting quantum computer, quadrupling qubit density within the same dilution refrigerator as their 64-qubit predecessor
Xanadu's Aurora photonic quantum computer networks 35 photonic chips across four server racks using 13 km of fiber optics at room temperature
D-Wave completes calibration and benchmarking of its 4,400-plus-qubit Advantage2 annealing quantum processor
QpiAI launches QpiAI-Indus, India's first full-stack 25-qubit superconducting quantum computer, under India's National Quantum Mission
Silicon Quantum Computing publishes an 11-qubit silicon processor in Nature with two-qubit gate fidelities up to 99.99% that improve as qubit count scales
AWS Braket adds new hardware backends for on-demand access
IonQ signs a $54.5 million contract with the Air Force Research Laboratory for quantum networking research, its largest 2024 US quantum award
DARPA selects Quantinuum to advance to Stage A of its Quantum Benchmarking Initiative
NERSC at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory opens cloud access to IBM quantum computers through a new Quantum Innovation Center
DARPA advances 11 companies, including IBM, IonQ and Quantinuum, to Stage B of its Quantum Benchmarking Initiative
Chinese Academy of Sciences researchers deliver a 504-qubit superconducting chip to QuantumCTek to build a cloud-accessible quantum computer with China Telecom
Origin Quantum's Wukong computer surpasses 20 million cumulative remote visits from users in 139 countries since its January 2024 launch
China Telecom Quantum Group and QuantumCTek put the Zuchongzhi superconducting quantum computer into commercial operation via the Tianyan cloud platform
Origin Quantum unveils its fourth-generation Wukong-180 quantum computer, after its predecessor logged about 50 million cloud accesses from over 160 countries
Pasqal delivers two 100+-qubit neutral-atom quantum computers to GENCI/CEA in France and FZJ in Germany under the EuroHPC-funded HPCQS project
IQM opens its first quantum data centre in Munich, Germany, with plans to host up to 12 quantum computers for industry customers
EuroHPC JU and IQM sign a procurement contract for the Euro-Q-Exa superconducting quantum computer to be hosted in Germany
EuroHPC JU and Qilimanjaro Quantum Tech sign a procurement contract for the MareNostrum Ona quantum annealer to be hosted in Spain
UK's National Quantum Computing Centre awards contracts to seven companies to deliver quantum computing testbeds under a £30 million programme
Australian and Queensland governments commit $940 million AUD to PsiQuantum to build a utility-scale fault-tolerant quantum computer near Brisbane
Fujitsu receives an order for a gate-based superconducting quantum computer from Japan's National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology
Yonsei University deploys the first IBM Quantum System One in South Korea, only the second such system on a university campus worldwide
IBM and RIKEN unveil the first IBM Quantum System Two deployed outside the United States, co-located with the Fugaku supercomputer in Kobe, Japan
Xanadu is selected for Canada's Quantum Champions Program and will receive up to CAD $23 million in federal funding

notes.author: E. Johnston
notes.headline: Error-correction and networking dominate the current preprint flow
notes.body: The newest quant-ph papers cluster around fault-tolerant codes and entanglement distribution rather than raw qubit records. That shift, from building bigger chips to making existing ones reliable, is the tell that the field is moving from demonstration toward engineering. Watch whether the US share of these papers holds as Chinese groups publish more on the same problems.

notes.author: E. Johnston
notes.headline: Below-threshold error correction is the milestone that actually matters
notes.body: Google's Willow result and IBM's stated path to 100k physical qubits are the two anchors here. The qubit-count headlines matter less than whether error rates fall as systems grow, which is the thing that decides if any of this scales. This stage has no live feed by design, so treat the five entries as a curated watchlist, not a census.

notes.author: E. Johnston
notes.headline: Procurement is still government and cloud, not end users
notes.body: DARPA's benchmarking program and EuroHPC's co-located systems show that the buyers are states and supercomputing centers, not commercial customers solving real problems. That is the honest state of adoption in 2026. The policy question is whether public procurement sustains the vendors long enough for a commercial market to appear.

notes.author: E. Johnston
notes.headline: US funding is visible, Chinese funding is not
notes.body: NSF awards give a clean read on US public spending on quantum, but there is no comparable public feed for China's NSFC or the provincial programs that carry much of the weight. Treat this stage as a floor on Western investment rather than a balanced comparison. The gap is a data-access problem, not evidence that Beijing is spending less.
